summary: I was born in Suwon, South Korea, which is where I spent most of my early life. Although I lived abroad for some years, Suwon has always been my home base. It holds many memories of my family and childhood before I moved to study in other countries.

South Korea is my home country where I was born and spent the majority of my life outside of my years abroad. Even though I lived in India and New Zealand for a total of about eight years, I always returned to South Korea. Leaving South Korea to study in the United States was a big step, especially leaving behind my family and the familiar comforts like my mom's cooking.

When I was very young, I studied in India and lived there for about four years. It was one of the first places I experienced life outside of South Korea. Those early years in India helped shape my perspective before I later moved to New Zealand for high school.

I spent my high school years in New Zealand, living there for about four years. It was an important time in my education and personal growth before I moved to the United States. New Zealand was a significant chapter in my life, bridging my early years in India and my later studies in America.

I came to the United States on August 19, 2013, to study mechanical engineering at the University of Minnesota. I chose the U.S. because of its better education system and opportunities. Leaving behind my family and friends in South Korea was difficult, but I brought with me essentials and my American dream. Working my first job here gave me a new appreciation for my dad's hard work and motivated me to succeed so I can support my family.

I enrolled at the University of Minnesota to study mechanical engineering after coming to the United States. This university became the place where I pursued my academic and career goals starting in 2013. It represents the opportunity I sought for a better education and the foundation for my future success.
